title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Kumozawa explains a miraculous sequence of events involving Mukemizu, Itô, and Utsugi that resulted in a bizarre crime scene. Mukemizu intended to decapitate both Itô and Utsugi simultaneously using a large knife. However, an anomaly occurred during the strike: as the blade passed through Itô's neck, his head momentarily remained on the blade's surface before the blade continued its path to decapitate Haya. Due to the extreme speed of the event and Newton's third law, Itô's head was pushed off the blade and landed vertically on the cut surface of the body.

Kumozawa posits that because the decapitation took less than a hundredth of a second, Itô's brain cells and sensory nerves remained briefly alive. He suggests that the cold temperature of the blade temporarily paralyzed the cells, and a temperature difference between the nerves created an electromotive force that allowed the neural fibers of Itô's head to connect with the nerves of Utsugi's body. This created a temporary "Frankenstein" entity, where Utsugi's body was controlled by Itô's consciousness.

Upon waking in this new body and seeing Maki holding a knife, Itô was gripped by extreme fear. He instinctively fled into the "Gate of Hell" and locked the door from the inside. Because Itô was left-handed, he used the left hand of Utsugi's body to lock the door, which caused the right-handed body's left hand to spasm. Eventually, exhausted and unable to survive without connected blood vessels, Itô died. This caused the head to separate from the body, creating the illusion of a locked-room crime scene.
